name: Threadfin Bream
description: Small, pinkish fish with sweet-tasting meat, commonly found in coastal fishing grounds
location: Shelf areas of Batangas and Quezon coasts.

name: Spotted Rabbitfish
description: A commercially important rabbitfish species found in seagrass beds and coral reef areas. A popular food fish in coastal communities.
location: Coastal waters of Batangas, especially areas with seagrass and shallow reefs.

name: Dilis
description: Small, schooling fish found near the coast, often used for fish paste and dried fish products.
location: Coastal waters of Cavite, Batangas, and Quezon, including Manila Bay.

name: Giant Trevally
description: A prized game fish found in Taal Lake, known for its delicious meat. It's the freshwater-adapted form of the marine Giant Trevally and iconic to Batangas.

name: Bigeye Scad
description: A schooling pelagic fish found in coastal waters and near reefs. Common in small-scale fisheries.
location: Coastal waters of Batangas and Quezon.

name: Nuidbranchs
description: Anilao, Batangas is globally recognized for its colorful nudibranchs. These sea slugs are popular among divers and marine photographers.

name: Whale Shark
description: The world's largest fish, known for its gentle nature. While not a permanent resident, whale sharks visit Quezon and Batangas waters during migration, attracting researchers and tourists.
location: Offshore waters of Quezon and Batangas (seasonal/migratory).

name: Green Sea Turtle
description: Large marine turtles that nest on CALABARZON beaches, especially in Quezon and Batangas. Protected due to their ecological importance.
location: Coastal nesting beaches of Quezon and Batangas.

name: Slipmouth
description: Small, silvery fish with protrusible mouths. Common in shallow waters and estuaries, often caught for food and dried products.
location: Manila Bay, and coastal waters of Cavite, Batangas, and Quezon.

name: Giant Clam
description: Large, colorful clams found in coral reefs. They help filter water and support reef health. Often protected in marine sanctuaries.
location: Coral reefs of Batangas and Quezon.

name: Tawilis
description: A small freshwater sardine found only in Taal Lake. It is culturally significant and unique as the world’s only freshwater sardine.

name: Yellowtail Snapper
description: A brightly colored snapper prized for its mild, flaky meat.
location: Coral reefs and rocky bottoms along Batangas coast.

name: Silver Perch
description: Also known as Ayungin, a native freshwater fish valued for its taste.

name: Striped Sea Perch
description: Common reef fish with distinct stripes, popular in local markets.

name: Spotted Scat
description: Known for its adaptability to both fresh and brackish waters.

name: Barred Knifejaw
description: A reef fish with a strong beak used for crushing shells.

name: Giant Trevally
description: Large predator fish valued by anglers and in cuisine.

name: Blue Tang
description: Popular reef fish known for its bright blue coloration.

name: Blacktip Grouper
description: Small grouper species with distinctive black-tipped fins.

name: Butterflyfish
description: Colorful reef fish often seen in pairs, feeding on coral polyps.

name: Red Snapper
description: Highly prized for firm, white flesh and mild flavor.

name: Asian Swamp Eel
description: Elongated eel-like fish commonly found in freshwater swamps.

name: Mudskipper
description: Unique fish capable of walking on land and breathing air.

name: Scad
description: Small schooling fish important in commercial fisheries.

name: Freshwater Climbing Perch
description: Fish known for its ability to breathe air and move on land.

name: Roughskin Catfish
description: Common catfish with rough skin found in brackish waters.

name: Needlefish
description: Slender fish with long jaws, often seen near the surface.

name: Threadfin Bream
description: Colorful fish popular in markets and for cooking.

name: Siganid
description: Also called rabbitfish, often seen grazing on algae.
